documentation.file_docstring: Plugin Hook Runner Provides utilities for executing plugin lifecycle hooks with proper error handling, priority ordering, and async support.
inline_comments: Re-export types for consumers
Get hooks for a specific hook name, sorted by priority (higher first).
Create a hook runner for a specific registry.
Keep the first defined override so higher-priority hooks win.
Run a hook that doesn't return a value (fire-and-forget style). All handlers are executed in parallel for performance.
Run a hook that can return a modifying result. Handlers are executed sequentially in priority order, and results are merged.
Run a sequential claim hook where the first `{ handled: true }` result wins.
Agent Hooks
Run before_model_resolve hook. Allows plugins to override provider/model before model resolution.
Run before_prompt_build hook. Allows plugins to inject context and system prompt before prompt submission.
Run before_agent_start hook. Legacy compatibility hook that combines model resolve + prompt build phases.
Run agent_end hook. Allows plugins to analyze completed conversations. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Run llm_input hook. Allows plugins to observe the exact input payload sent to the LLM. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Run llm_output hook. Allows plugins to observe the exact output payload returned by the LLM. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Run before_compaction hook.
Run after_compaction hook.
Run before_reset hook. Fired when /new or /reset clears a session, before messages are lost. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Message Hooks
Run inbound_claim hook. Allows plugins to claim an inbound event before commands/agent dispatch.
Run message_received hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Run message_sending hook. Allows plugins to modify or cancel outgoing messages. Runs sequentially.
Run message_sent hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Tool Hooks
Run before_tool_call hook. Allows plugins to modify or block tool calls. Runs sequentially.
Run after_tool_call hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).

Run tool_result_persist hook. This hook is intentionally synchronous: it runs in hot paths where session transcripts are appended synchronously. Handlers are executed sequentially in priority order (higher first). Each handler may return `{ message }` to replace the message passed to the next handler.
oxlint-disable-next-line typescript/no-explicit-any
Guard against accidental async handlers (this hook is sync-only).
oxlint-disable-next-line typescript/no-explicit-any
Message Write Hooks

Run before_message_write hook. This hook is intentionally synchronous: it runs on the hot path where session transcripts are appended synchronously. Handlers are executed sequentially in priority order (higher first). If any handler returns { block: true }, the message is NOT written to the session JSONL and we return immediately. If a handler returns { message }, the modified message replaces the original for subsequent handlers and the final write.
oxlint-disable-next-line typescript/no-explicit-any
Guard against accidental async handlers (this hook is sync-only).
oxlint-disable-next-line typescript/no-explicit-any
If any handler blocks, return immediately.
If handler provided a modified message, use it for subsequent handlers.
If message was modified by any handler, return it.
Session Hooks
Run session_start hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Run session_end hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Run subagent_spawning hook. Runs sequentially so channel plugins can deterministically provision session bindings.
Run subagent_delivery_target hook. Runs sequentially so channel plugins can deterministically resolve routing.
Run subagent_spawned hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Run subagent_ended hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Gateway Hooks
Run gateway_start hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Run gateway_stop hook. Runs in parallel (fire-and-forget).
Utility
Check if any hooks are registered for a given hook name.
Get count of registered hooks for a given hook name.
Agent hooks
Message hooks
Tool hooks
Message write hooks
Session hooks
Gateway hooks
Utility
